# Handover — Native Object Tools release setup

**Date:** 2026-04-19
**Purpose:** End-of-day handover so the next conversation can resume without relying on an auto-generated summary of today's chat.

## How to use this document

Open a fresh chat tomorrow, attach this file (or paste its contents), and ask to continue from here. The assistant will pick up where we left off.

## Decisions settled — formerly open questions, now resolved

The six questions originally posed in this section were adjudicated at the start of the 2026-04-20 session. Their resolutions are recorded below. The full decision trail is preserved in the Changelog at the end of this document.

### 1. `Fnd_FCS_BuildComponent` ships with the released component.

Both the method and its companion template `Resources/templatebuildApp.xml` ship on Koala and Platypus. The rationale is that downstream users must be able to rebuild the component against their own signing identity; the method cannot function without the template. Consequences:

- The `[conditional]` marker against `Resources/templatebuildApp.xml` in the exclusions manifest is lifted. The template is not stripped.
- Task #2 (compiler module separation) is simplified: `Fnd_FCS_BuildComponent` does not need to be segregated into `Compiler_Prototyping.4dm`.
- Task #3 (exclusion manifest) now has exactly one definite entry (`Resources/OTr_OTBlockMethods.json`) and no conditional entries arising from this question.

### 2. `LegacyDocumentation/` is removed.

Removal is via plain `git rm -r LegacyDocumentation` folded into the Step 0 reorganisation commit. No history rewrite is required; prior commits retain the folder for archaeological purposes. A read-only compressed disk image snapshot of the repository taken on 2026-04-20 via Disk Utility preserves the folder outside of git in any case.

### 3. Four methods reference `"Resources/1 Corinthians 1.docx"` as a hard-coded path.

The methods are:

- `____Make_Phase16_OTBlobFixtures`
- `____Test_OTr_Master`
- `____Test_Phase_16b_OTBlobDeep`
- `OTr_w_Phase16Diag`

All four must have their path literals updated in lockstep with the `git mv` of the fixture file, in the same commit. A grep of `Project/` for the literal path string will be run at the start of Step 0 as a formality, to confirm no additional references exist beyond these four.

### 4. The test fixture relocates to `Examples/`, not `Project/TestFixtures/`.

`Examples/` already exists for this purpose, and introducing a new top-level `Project/TestFixtures/` folder would be gratuitous. The revised move is `git mv "Resources/1 Corinthians 1.docx" "Examples/1 Corinthians 1.docx"`.

### 5. `.gitignore` uses an unanchored pattern for `Secret Key.txt`.

The pattern `Secret Key.txt` (no leading slash, no path prefix) matches at any depth. Current `.gitignore` to be inspected at the start of Step 0 and adjusted if the existing pattern is path-anchored. The file has been gitignored throughout its life in `Resources/`, so no history cleanup is expected to be required.

### 6. Runner carries the purpose-specific label `native-object-tools-release`.

Added via GitHub's Settings → Actions → Runners page on 2026-04-20. No runner restart required; the edit took effect immediately. The runner's label set is now `self-hosted`, `macOS`, `X64`, `iMac-Pro`, `native-object-tools-release`. The release workflow's `runs-on:` line should therefore read:

```yaml
runs-on: [self-hosted, macOS, X64, iMac-Pro, native-object-tools-release]
```

The label serves both as documentation of intent in the workflow file and as a binding mechanism should additional repository-scoped runners ever be registered on this iMac Pro.

---

## Where we are in the overall project

Native Object Tools is a native 4D replacement for the legacy Object Tools plugin, targeting 4D v19 LTS minimum. The project is nearing readiness for release. The work currently in progress is **setting up the release pipeline** — the end-to-end automation that will produce signed, notarised installers for 4D v19, v20, and v21, in both `OTr_` (native style) and `OT ` (legacy-compatible) variants.

The governing design document is `Release/README.md` in this repository. That document is authoritative; if anything in this handover conflicts with it, the README wins.

## Architecture, in one paragraph

Three Git branches: `Echidna` (full development, `OTr_` prefix), `Koala` (release-ready `OTr_`, stripped of dev-only material), `Platypus` (release-ready `OT ` prefix, produced from Koala via the existing Renamer project). A self-hosted GitHub Actions runner on the iMac Pro executes the release workflow, which performs: stageKoala → stagePlatypus → compile (×6 variants) → build (×6) → sign → package → notarise → zip → publish. Final artefacts: three DMG files (one per 4D version, each containing both the `OTr_` and `OT ` components) plus zipped equivalents for Windows.

## Accomplishments to date

1. **Runner installed and running as a LaunchAgent.** Reboot-tested successfully. Started with PID 736 after boot. Location: `/Users/runner/actions-runner/`. Registered to `github.com/WayneStewart/nativeObjectTools`. Labels: `self-hosted`, `macOS`, `X64`, `iMac-Pro`.

2. **Runner account provisioned.** Separate `runner` macOS user, auto-login configured, no iCloud, no Claude app, no VS Code, GitHub Desktop yes, no 1Password. Developer ID `.p12` imported into `runner`'s keychain. SSH deploy key added to repository (read access).

3. **Security settings discussion started but NOT YET APPLIED.** See Steps 1–5 below.

4. **Step 0 complete** (commit `257b3cd`, 2026-04-20). Reserved-folder reorganisation executed on Echidna. Repo is clean.

## Next steps — pick up here

### ~~Step 0: Reserved-folder reorganisation~~ — COMPLETE (commit `257b3cd`, 2026-04-20)

Executed as a single atomic commit on Echidna. Final folder dispositions:

- `Documentation/Methods/` — only item in Documentation/. Ships.
- `Resources/InfoPlist.json` — ships. Required runtime.
- `Resources/InfoPlist.strings` — ships. Required runtime.
- `Resources/OTr.xlf` — ships. Component constants/localisation.
- `Resources/images/` — ships. Runtime assets for `OTr_z_Xxx` image methods.
- `Resources/OTr_OTBlockMethods.json` — ships on Echidna; STRIP on Koala/Platypus (exclusions.md entry).
- `Resources/templatebuildApp.xml` — ships on Koala/Platypus (per Decision #1).
- `Extras/` — deleted entirely.
- `LegacyDocumentation/` — deleted entirely (per Decision #2).
- `Specifications/` — at root, maintainer-only.
- `DevNotes/` — at root, maintainer-only (Codex-Live-Testing.md, Example Logs/, Sessions/, PDFs/).
- `Secret Key.txt` — at root, gitignored (unanchored pattern confirmed pre-existing).
- `Examples/1 Corinthians 1.docx` — test fixture (per Decision #4); four test methods updated to `Get 4D folder(Database folder)+"Examples"+Folder separator+"1 Corinthians 1.docx"` matching the pattern established in `OTr_w_GuyBlobExport`.

Reconnaissance confirmed: exactly four method files held the old path; `.gitignore` already used an unanchored pattern — no adjustment required. `LegacyDocumentation/legacyExamples/` was gitignored and left as an untracked filesystem directory.

Tasks completed by this commit: Task #10, Task #11, Task #12.

### Step 1: Finish the Echidna ruleset

In the GitHub web UI, under Settings → Rules → Rulesets, the Echidna ruleset was partially configured. The two ticks are already correct:
- "Restrict deletions" — ticked
- "Block force pushes" — ticked

**Still needed on that ruleset:**
- Configure "Target branches" — click "Add target" → "Include by pattern" → enter `Echidna` (or "Include default branch" if Echidna is the default). Without a target, the ruleset applies to nothing.
- Leave all other rule checkboxes unticked (do not require signed commits, PRs, status checks, linear history, etc.)
- Save.

### Step 2: Create a second ruleset for release branches

- Name: `Release branches`
- Enforcement: Active
- Bypass list: add yourself (Repository admin role)
- Target branches: add `Koala`, add `Platypus` (separate entries, or brace pattern if supported)
- All rule checkboxes **unticked** — these branches are intentionally force-pushable and deletable by the release process

### Step 3: Configure Settings → Actions → General

Three sub-settings to adjust:
- **Actions permissions:** "Allow WayneStewart, and select non-WayneStewart, actions and reusable workflows"; tick "Allow actions created by GitHub".
- **Fork pull request workflows from outside collaborators:** set to "Require approval for all outside collaborators". This is the single most important safety setting for a self-hosted runner.
- **Workflow permissions:** "Read repository contents and packages permissions" (not "Read and write"). Elevated permissions will be granted per-workflow via explicit `permissions:` blocks.
- Click Save.

### Step 4: Verify deploy key is read-only

Settings → Deploy keys → confirm the runner's SSH key does NOT have "Allow write access" ticked. Writes will go via the automatic `GITHUB_TOKEN`, scoped per-workflow, rather than the long-lived deploy key.

### Step 5: Smoke-test the runner

Before writing the real release workflow, create a trivial workflow that does nothing but check out the repo and run `4d --version` (or similar). Purpose: confirm the runner takes jobs, the checkout works, and 4D is invocable from the workflow environment. The real release workflow is too large to debug end-to-end; a 10-line smoke test catches configuration errors cheaply.

### Step 6: Task #3 — the exclusion manifest

After the runner is smoke-tested, the next substantive work is producing `Release/exclusions.md` — the keystone manifest that lists every file and pattern to strip when transitioning from Echidna to Koala. All subsequent implementation tasks depend on this being complete.

**Task #3 is now unblocked** — the `LegacyDocumentation/` question was settled in favour of removal (Decision #2), and the folder comes out in the Step 0 reorganisation commit rather than being stripped at Koala/Platypus staging time.

## Runner first-time setup — items still to verify

From the README's "First-time setup on the runner" checklist:

- [ ] 4D v19 installed at `/Applications/4D/19/...` and launched once under `runner` to accept licence
- [ ] 4D v20 installed at `/Applications/4D/20/...` and launched once under `runner`
- [ ] 4D v21 installed at `/Applications/4D/21/...` and launched once under `runner`
- [ ] DMG Canvas installed with CLI (`dmgcanvas`) on runner's PATH
- [ ] `notarytool` keychain profile created under `runner`:
  ```
  xcrun notarytool store-credentials "OTr-Notary" \
    --apple-id "your-apple-id@example.com" \
    --team-id "H8FSU88B4T" \
    --password "app-specific-password"
  ```
  (App-specific password from appleid.apple.com, not your Apple ID password.)

These can be done in parallel with the GitHub-side work; they are not blocking.

## Changelog

### 2026-04-19 — document created
End-of-day handover drafted after the evening session. Six open questions recorded; Step 0 reorganisation plan drafted in preliminary form (with `Project/TestFixtures/` as the fixture destination and `Fnd_FCS_BuildComponent` template disposition conditional). Runner installed, provisioned, and labelled `self-hosted, macOS, X64, iMac-Pro`. GitHub-side configuration (Steps 1–5) outlined but not yet executed.

### 2026-04-20 — open questions resolved
All six open questions adjudicated at the start of the morning session. Summary of rulings:

1. **`Fnd_FCS_BuildComponent` ships** on Koala and Platypus; `Resources/templatebuildApp.xml` ships alongside. Template removed from the exclusions strip list. Task #2 simplified accordingly.
2. **`LegacyDocumentation/` removed** via `git rm -r` in the Step 0 commit; no history rewrite.
3. **Four methods** reference the hard-coded fixture path: `____Make_Phase16_OTBlobFixtures`, `____Test_OTr_Master`, `____Test_Phase_16b_OTBlobDeep`, `OTr_w_Phase16Diag`. Grep to be run at start of Step 0 to confirm completeness.
4. **Test fixture relocates to `Examples/`** (not `Project/TestFixtures/`), reusing the existing Examples folder.
5. **Unanchored `.gitignore` pattern** `Secret Key.txt` — current pattern to be inspected and adjusted if path-anchored.
6. **Runner label `native-object-tools-release` added** via GitHub web UI; edit took effect immediately without runner restart. Full label set now `self-hosted, macOS, X64, iMac-Pro, native-object-tools-release`.

Additional note: a read-only compressed disk image of the entire repository was taken via Disk Utility before any reorganisation, as a rollback point. No changes to repository files have been made in this session; the Step 0 commit has not yet been executed.

### 2026-04-20 — Step 0 executed

Reserved-folder reorganisation committed as `257b3cd` on Echidna. 92 files changed. Reconnaissance confirmed exactly four method files held the old fixture path; all four updated to use `Get 4D folder(Database folder)+"Examples"+Folder separator+"1 Corinthians 1.docx"` (pattern matching `OTr_w_GuyBlobExport`). `.gitignore` unanchored pattern for `Secret Key.txt` confirmed pre-existing — no adjustment required. `LegacyDocumentation/legacyExamples/` was fully gitignored; left as untracked filesystem directory. Repository working tree is clean. Next: Steps 1–5 (GitHub-side ruleset and Actions configuration).
